name: React TypeScript
description: Version-aware React TypeScript production guidance. Use only when project detection and an active binding prove this technology is in the task scope; trigger on editing TSX components, hooks, context, JSX typing, or React rendering behavior.
purpose: Keep component, hook, accessibility, and type-system work aligned with detected React and TypeScript releases.
supported_versions: Detected project versions supported by the selected upstream release line; no version is assumed by the base skill.
activation_conditions: Project stack manifest marks detection true.
Confidence is HIGH or MEDIUM; LOW requires confirmation.
Task intersects an applicable package, path, and task type in the active binding.
deactivation_conditions: Technology evidence is removed or becomes contradictory.
Binding scope no longer intersects the task.
Detected release is unsupported or compatibility is unresolved.
confidence_requirement: HIGH or MEDIUM for normal routing; LOW requires explicit confirmation; NONE is inactive.
task_triggers: editing TSX components, hooks, context, JSX typing, or React rendering behavior
reviewing React architecture or TypeScript boundaries
required_inputs: Detected installed/platform version for version-sensitive work.
Task acceptance criteria and relevant Standard Skill context.
expected_outputs: Change or review aligned to the detected release and repository conventions.
Validation evidence scoped to affected paths.
Manifest, overlay, and memory updates only when durable facts changed.
rules: Preserve hook ordering and render purity.
Prefer explicit domain types and narrow unknown values at boundaries.
Match the repository component and state ownership conventions.
blockers: No active binding or no task/scope intersection.
Only comment, filename, or model-memory evidence exists.
A requested rule conflicts with the Constitution or required Standard Skills.
React or TypeScript major cannot be established for a version-sensitive API.
patterns: Small typed components with explicit ownership.
Accessible semantic controls and deterministic state transitions.
anti_patterns: Assume the newest upstream release is installed.
Apply the skill across unrelated monorepo packages.
Copy upstream documentation or upgrade dependencies without task authority.
Use effects to derive values that can be computed during render.
Silence type errors with broad any casts.
validation: Run the repository TypeScript check, lint, component tests, and accessibility checks in affected scope.
completion_criteria: Relevant tests/lints/checks pass or an evidence-backed exception is recorded.
Detection and activation manifests remain synchronized.
No project fact or secret leaked into Layer A.
official_source_policy: Use only official upstream documentation for version-sensitive claims; record checked date and distinguish verified, deprecated, migration, and unverified guidance in UPSTREAM.md.
memory_read_policy: Read project memory only for the affected module and decisions; never treat stale memory as stronger than repository evidence.
memory_write_policy: Write only durable verified decisions, constraints, failures, or patterns; never write secrets or speculative versions.
project_overlay_policy: Project-specific paths, versions, conventions, and narrowing rules belong in Layer B; overlays cannot weaken base, security, compatibility, or production rules.
update_policy: Re-verify official sources after detected major upgrades, deprecations, platform changes, or conflicting repository evidence; do not force dependency upgrades.
deprecation_policy: Mark deprecated before disabling, name the replacement or migration path, keep compatibility warnings, and deactivate bindings when evidence disappears.
scope_policy: Apply only to packages and paths declared in an active binding; repository-wide scope requires evidence that every included package uses the technology.
